name: UNICEF USA
mission: Over eight decades, the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) has built an unprecedented global support system for the world's children. UNICEF relentlessly works day in and day out to deliver the essentials that give every child an equitable chance in life: health care and immunizations, safe water and sanitation, nutrition, education, emergency relief and more. UNICEF USA advances the global mission of UNICEF by rallying the American public to support the world's most vulnerable children. Together, we have helped save more children's lives than any other humanitarian organization.
theoryOfChange: UNICEF USA, through its Impact Fund for Children, has co-developed a 'Child-Lens Investment Framework'. This framework serves as a roadmap for investors to frame strategies and direct capital toward advancing child outcomes and minimizing child harm. It is described as a 'central guiding document' that includes core principles, a case for investment, an analysis of the global market landscape, and a description of the Child-Lens Investment ecosystem. Additionally, the Impact Fund reports impact through an 'Input/Output/Outcomes Framework' and by mapping each acceleration to UNICEF to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

grantsData.purpose: SEE PART V UNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN CENTRAL AMERICA AND THE CARIBBEAN, UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICANT HUMANITARIAN NEEDS RELATED TO MIGRATION, VIOLENCE, INTERNAL DISPLACEMENT, FOOD INSECURITY AND POVERTY.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.UNICEF'S WORK IN EAST ASIA IS FOCUSED ON EMERGENCY RESPONSE, AS OVER 1.6 BILLION PEOPLE IN THE REGION HAVE BEEN AFFECTED BY NATURAL DISASTERS SINCE 2000. THE REGION IS ALSO SIGNIFICANTLY AFFECTED BY THE NEGATIVE IMPACTS OF CLIMATE CHANGE. CYCLONES, FLOODS AND DROUGHTS ARE MORE FREQUENT AND MORE INTENSE THAN EVER BEFORE. UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON MITIGATING HARM IN THE WAKE OF NATURAL DISASTERS AND STRENGTHENING THE REGION'S HEALTH, NUTRITION, SOCIAL PROTECTION, AND CHILD PROTECTION INFRASTRUCTURE.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN THE WAKE OF THE WAR IN UKRAINE, FOR EXAMPLE, UNICEF IS ON THE GROUND MEETING URGENT AND ESCALATING NEEDS FOR SAFE WATER, HEALTH CARE, NUTRITION AND PROTECTION.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.UNICEF IS WORKING TO REACH THE TENS OF MILLIONS OF CHILDREN ACROSS THE MIDDLE EAST AND NORTH AFRICA ARE HEAVILY IMPACTED BY CONFLICT, THE CLIMATE CHANGE CRISIS AND OTHER PROTRACTED EMERGENCIES, INCLUDING, FOR EXAMPLE, IN AFGHANISTAN, WHERE 28.3 MILLION PEOPLE, MORE THAN 65 PERCENT OF THE POPULATION, ARE PROJECTED TO BE IN NEED OF HUMANITARIAN ASSISTANCE IN 2023.
SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN THE WAKE OF THE WAR IN UKRAINE, FOR EXAMPLE, UNICEF IS ON THE GROUND MEETING URGENT AND ESCALATING NEEDS FOR SAFE WATER, HEALTH CARE, NUTRITION AND PROTECTION.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN SOUTH AMERICA, UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICANT HUMANITARIAN NEEDS RELATED TO MIGRATION, VIOLENCE, INTERNAL DISPLACEMENT, FOOD INSECURITY AND POVERTY.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE. UNICEF'S WORK IN SOUTH ASIA FOCUSES ON CHILD PROTECTION, EMERGENCIES, INCLUDING RELIEF FROM FLOODING IN PAKISTAN, EDUCATION, AND DELIVERY OF CRITICAL SUPPLIES TO COMBAT THE COVID-19 PANDEMIC.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE. UNICEF IS WORKING TO DELIVER HUMANITARIAN ASSISTANCE TO THOSE IMPACTED BY FOOD CRISES, SCREENING AND TREATING CHILDREN SUFFERING FROM MALNUTRITION AND PROVIDING SAFE WATER, SANITATION AND HYGIENE SERVICES TO COMMUNITIES WHERE THESE BASIC RESOURCES ARE IN SHORT SUPPLY, AMONG OTHER INTERVENTIONS.

judge_issues.message: The rationale states that the organization earns a GMG score of 70/100, but the assigned score is 70. This could be rephrased to avoid confusion.

The rationale states: "Alignment 31/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, education global cause area". This could be clearer by explicitly stating that the alignment score is 31 out of 50 due to a medium Muslim donor fit and the education global cause area.
Evaluation source domain 'charitywatch.org' is not a known trusted evaluator

The organization provides various donation options for humanitarian crises.
UNICEF USA offers multiple giving methods for general charitable support.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a GMG score of 70/100 due to its high financial transparency and efficient program delivery, though its alignment score reflects a medium fit for specific donor segments.
areas_for_improvement: Relatively short working capital reserve of 2.8 months
Medium donor alignment for specific niche audience requirements

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium alignment for Muslim donors seeking sadaqah-eligible opportunities in global education and health [6][7]. While it addresses urgent global causes, the alignment score of 31/50 reflects a balanced track record between broad international appeal and specific donor preferences [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: UNICEF USA demonstrates high impact through capacity building, maintaining a program expense ratio of 87.6% and a high data confidence level [1][3]. Its financial health is evidenced by a 97.0/100 rating from third-party evaluators, though it maintains a lean 2.8 months of working capital [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: UNICEF USA supports global initiatives to end preventable maternal and child deaths while promoting the health and development of children worldwide.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 87.6% ensuring the majority of funds reach field programs
Strong financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 97.0/100
Efficient fundraising operations with a cost of only $0.12 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: UNICEF USA operates as a major humanitarian organization focusing on health, nutrition, and water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) programs [1]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $720,404,955 to support its global mission [3].

alignment.explanation: This charity is a strong fit for donors interested in <cite id="19">global humanitarian relief</cite> and education. While it is <cite id="20">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE</cite>, it serves highly vulnerable populations in crisis zones.
alignment.improvement: Adding a dedicated page to clarify the <cite id="21">Sadaqah impact</cite> for Muslim donors could improve alignment with faith-based giving priorities.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="7">97.0/100 from Charity Navigator rating</cite> and <cite id="6">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>. It employs <cite id="9">95% independent board members</cite> and undergoes regular audited financial reviews.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility further, the charity could publish more consistent beneficiary counts to allow for <cite id="16">detailed cost-per-beneficiary assessments</cite>.

impact.explanation: Operations show a <cite id="3">87.6% program expense ratio</cite> with a focus on <cite id="17">capacity building across 190 countries</cite>. They track <cite id="18">specific outcome metrics</cite> through Candid to measure long-term success.
impact.improvement: Building <cite id="13">working capital reserves</cite> toward a 3-12 month target would improve long-term financial resilience.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcomes and a strong theory of change, though cost-per-beneficiary data is not consistently published.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: UNICEF USA uses a capacity-building model to strengthen national systems for health, education, and child protection, ensuring sustainable impact beyond immediate relief.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that large-scale interventions actually improve child survival rates and educational outcomes.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Global humanitarian needs for children consistently outpace available funding, especially in conflict zones.
strategic_priorities: Emergency response in Gaza and Ukraine

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="17">unmatched reach in 190+ countries</cite> and a massive grantmaking capacity.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Stronger focus on women and girls' empowerment
similar_organizations.name: CARE USA

strengths.detail: The organization holds a <cite id="7">97/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting rigorous governance and financial integrity.
UNICEF USA operates with a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.12 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than many large-scale humanitarian peers.
The charity is overseen by a <cite id="8">board of 20 members</cite>, with <cite id="9">95% of board members being independent</cite> to ensure objective oversight.

strengths_deep_dive: UNICEF USA demonstrates a high capacity for large-scale grantmaking, distributing <cite id="10">over $1.6 billion in foreign grants</cite> to support international programs. Their financial health is bolstered by a <cite id="11">3-year revenue CAGR of 27.4%</cite>, indicating strong growth and donor trust. The organization also maintains a <cite id="12">perfect 100 score in Culture and Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator, highlighting a healthy internal organizational environment.
rich_narrative.summary: UNICEF USA serves as a vital fundraising and advocacy arm for the global United Nations Children's Fund, focusing on ending preventable child deaths. The organization manages a massive scale of operations, reporting <cite id="1">$829,050,932 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. Their work spans critical sectors including <cite id="2">health, nutrition, water, and education</cite> across more than 190 countries and territories. By leveraging a capacity-building model, they strengthen local systems to provide long-term stability for children in crisis zones. The charity maintains a high level of financial efficiency, directing <cite id="3">87.6% of its total expenses</cite> toward program services. This ensures that the vast majority of resources reach field operations in regions like <cite id="4">Ukraine, Gaza, and Pakistan</cite>. Their fundraising model is notably lean, spending only <cite id="5">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions. This efficiency allows the organization to maximize the impact of every dollar donated for humanitarian relief. While they operate as a massive institution, they maintain high transparency standards, evidenced by a <cite id="6">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. This level of disclosure provides donors with clear insights into their strategic goals and outcome tracking. The organization's long history and global reach make it a cornerstone of international development and emergency response.
